newsSince: In May 2026 Azbil announced a ¥20B (32M-share, ~6.2%) buyback plus higher ordinary and commemorative dividends and FY2027 guidance (rev ¥315B, profit ¥35.3B), and actively repurchased shares through late May. No new building-automation/data-center contract news in the June 9-23 window; the ~+10% move reads as buyback support plus general re-rating.

sources.label: Simply Wall St - Azbil FY2026 results
Simply Wall St - Azbil buyback & dividend hike

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold. No new evidence since June 9; only post-May-13-results flow. Building automation remains a real but indirect thermal/facility node for data-center efficiency. Still mostly priced as a Japanese control co, so the latent-node discount persists; nothing changed the case either way.
verdicts.bull: BA segment strong; facility energy-efficiency demand grows with data-center buildout; still under-covered as a latent thermal node.
verdicts.neutral: Indirect exposure; valued as a Japan control company; modest revenue growth.
verdicts.bear: FY2026 revenue/earnings dipped slightly; +9.95% move is flow, not fundamentals; data-center linkage is thin.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Building automation retrofit + data-center facility efficiency demand
verdicts.primaryRisk: Margin decline; only indirect AI exposure
